name: parasomnia_event_capture
consequences: Immediate: Increased participant retention → Systemic: Enhanced data quality → Strategic: Improved research outcomes
options: Enhanced PSG on scheduled nights only
Dry-electrode EEG headbands and contact-free mattress sensors for continuous monitoring
Full PSG on escalation nights after technician-flagged events
review: Controls participant safety vs. researcher workload. Weakness: The options fail to consider the impact of technician fatigue on data quality.

name: participant_recruitment_strategy
consequences: Immediate: Increased recruitment rates → Systemic: Diversified participant pool → Strategic: Enhanced research generalizability
options: Recruitment through University Hospital Bonn sleep clinic and regional neurologist referrals only
Inclusion of online advertising and social media campaigns targeting sleep disorder communities
Targeted outreach to patient advocacy groups and support organizations for NREM parasomnias
review: Controls participant diversity vs. recruitment costs. Weakness: The options fail to consider the potential bias introduced by targeted online advertising.

name: data_sharing_protocol
consequences: Immediate: Increased data sharing → Systemic: Enhanced collaboration → Strategic: Improved research impact
options: Limited public sharing of de-identified physiological and sensor data only
Sharing of anonymized participant-level data with select research partners
Open-access publication of all raw and processed data under a Creative Commons license
review: Controls data accessibility vs. participant privacy. Weakness: The options fail to consider the potential risks associated with open-access data sharing.

name: participant_compensation
consequences: Immediate: Increased participant satisfaction → Systemic: Enhanced recruitment rates → Strategic: Improved research quality
options: Per-night compensation of €80 plus meals only
Additional incentives for participants with high event yields or difficult-to-study conditions
Guaranteed compensation of €1,000 per week for all participants
review: Controls participant satisfaction vs. recruitment costs. Weakness: The options fail to consider the potential impact on participant motivation and engagement.

name: staffing_model
consequences: Immediate: Increased staff productivity → Systemic: Enhanced research capacity → Strategic: Improved project sustainability
options: Rotating night shifts with a maximum of 3 technicians per shift only
Hiring additional research technicians to cover morning and afternoon shifts
Implementation of a 24/7 staffing model with dedicated day and night teams
review: Controls staff productivity vs. operational costs. Weakness: The options fail to consider the potential impact on staff burnout and turnover.

name: event-triage_tool_development
consequences: Immediate: Increased event detection accuracy → Systemic: Enhanced data analysis → Strategic: Improved research outcomes
options: Development of a simple rule-based system only
Implementation of a machine learning-based approach with human oversight
Integration of an expert system with deep learning capabilities
review: Controls event-triage tool effectiveness vs. development complexity. Weakness: The options fail to consider the potential impact on data quality and participant safety.

name: research_unit_expansion
consequences: Immediate: Increased research capacity → Systemic: Enhanced project sustainability → Strategic: Improved research impact
options: Expansion of the current facility to accommodate additional participants only
Construction of a new facility with enhanced features and capabilities
Implementation of a modular expansion plan with phased roll-out
review: Controls research unit capacity vs. operational costs. Weakness: The options fail to consider the potential impact on participant experience and staff productivity.

name: participant_selection_protocol
consequences: Immediate: Increased participant retention → Systemic: Enhanced data quality and generalizability → Strategic: Improved research credibility and publication potential
options: Structured clinical interview with collateral history, prior polysomnography, and exclusion checklist for targeted recruitment
Incentivized online survey and self-reported parasomnia episodes for efficient enrollment
Community outreach and partnerships with sleep clinics and neurologist networks for diverse participant pool
review: Controls participant selection bias vs. participant dropout. Weakness: The options fail to consider the potential impact of participant compensation on data quality and retention.

name: data_storage_and_retention_policy
consequences: Immediate: Reduced data loss and security risks → Systemic: Enhanced data sharing and collaboration opportunities → Strategic: Improved research reproducibility and transparency
options: Local NAS storage with nightly encrypted backup to university servers
Cloud-based storage with secure access controls and versioning
On-site data archiving with physical media storage for long-term preservation
review: Controls data security vs. data sharing. Weakness: The options fail to consider the potential impact of data retention on participant privacy and confidentiality.

name: research_unit_expansion_strategy
consequences: Immediate: Increased research capacity and participant enrollment → Systemic: Enhanced data collection and analysis opportunities → Strategic: Improved research productivity and publication potential
options: Phased expansion of existing facility with additional suites and equipment
New facility construction or acquisition for increased capacity and flexibility
Partnership-based collaboration with other research institutions for shared resources and expertise
review: Controls research unit growth vs. resource allocation. Weakness: The options fail to consider the potential impact of expansion on staff training, participant recruitment, and data quality.

name: participant_compensation_model
consequences: Immediate: Increased participant engagement and retention → Systemic: Enhanced data quality and generalizability → Strategic: Improved research credibility and publication potential
options: Flat rate per night with meals and transportation reimbursement
Tiered compensation based on participant participation and data quality
Incentivized online survey and self-reported parasomnia episodes for efficient enrollment
review: Controls participant compensation vs. participant dropout. Weakness: The options fail to consider the potential impact of compensation on participant motivation and research outcomes.

name: data_analysis_and_reporting_protocol
consequences: Immediate: Enhanced data insights and research findings → Systemic: Improved research reproducibility and transparency → Strategic: Improved research credibility and publication potential
options: Automated data analysis with machine learning algorithms
Human-centered data analysis with expert review and validation
Hybrid approach combining automated and human-centered analysis
review: Controls data analysis vs. data reporting. Weakness: The options fail to consider the potential impact of analysis on research conclusions and publication outcomes.

name: participant_informed_consent_process
consequences: Immediate: Enhanced participant understanding and informed decision-making → Systemic: Improved research ethics and compliance → Strategic: Improved research credibility and publication potential
options: Standardized consent form with clear language and explanation
Individualized consent process with tailored information and discussion
Informed consent through online survey or self-reported parasomnia episodes
review: Controls participant informed consent vs. participant dropout. Weakness: The options fail to consider the potential impact of consent on research ethics and compliance.

name: research_unit_operations_model
consequences: Immediate: Safe and efficient research operations → Systemic: Enhanced data collection and analysis opportunities → Strategic: Improved research productivity and publication potential
options: Standardized operating procedures with clear roles and responsibilities
Flexible operations model with adaptability to changing research needs
Hybrid approach combining standardized and flexible operations
review: Controls research unit operations vs. resource allocation. Weakness: The options fail to consider the potential impact of operations on staff training, participant recruitment, and data quality.

name: sleep_suite_design
consequences: Immediate: Increased participant comfort → Systemic: Enhanced data quality through reduced false alarm rates → Strategic: Improved participant retention and study scalability
options: Preserve original residential feel with minimal renovations
Implement subtle clinical design cues to optimize sleep environment
Radically transform the space into a fully immersive, high-tech sleep lab
review: Controls Participant comfort vs. Clinical-grade data quality. Weakness: The options fail to consider the impact of radical design changes on participant recruitment and retention rates.

name: participant_screening_protocol
consequences: Immediate: Reduced participant drop-off → Systemic: Improved data quality through enhanced participant selection → Strategic: Increased study generalizability and external validity
options: Adopt a streamlined, low-threshold screening process
Implement a more rigorous, multi-step screening protocol with increased exclusion criteria
Integrate AI-powered risk assessment tools to inform participant selection
review: Controls Participant recruitment vs. Data quality. Weakness: The options fail to consider the potential bias introduced by using AI-powered risk assessment tools.

name: data_storage_and_security_policy
consequences: Immediate: Ensured data integrity → Systemic: Protected participant privacy → Strategic: Maintained regulatory compliance and avoided reputational damage
options: Utilize standard, commercially available cloud storage solutions
Implement a custom-built on-premises storage solution with enhanced security features
Develop a decentralized, blockchain-based data storage system
review: Controls Data integrity vs. Participant privacy. Weakness: The options fail to consider the feasibility and scalability of implementing a decentralized data storage system.
